description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a series of rectangular photovoltaic panels mounted on a flat, gridded platform. The panels are arranged in a linear row, each panel slightly tilted to optimize sunlight capture, and supported by a visible structural frame. The platform beneath the panels appears to be made of metal, with a ribbed or slatted texture, likely for drainage and structural integrity. Several black box-like components are distributed along the platform, possibly housing electrical or mounting hardware. The overall structure is low-profile and elongated, suitable for placement on rooftops or flat urban surfaces. The design is utilitarian, emphasizing durability and efficient solar energy collection.
description: A low-profile, elongated solar panel array mounted on a ribbed metal platform with several black hardware components. The panels are arranged in a linear row and slightly tilted for optimal sunlight capture.
description_view_0: A side view showing the full length of the solar panel array and the ribbed platform, with the tilted panels and black hardware components clearly visible.
description_view_1: An angled end view highlighting the tilt of the solar panels and the depth of the supporting platform structure.
description_view_2: A rear side view showing the back of the platform and the alignment of the panels, with less detail visible on the panel surfaces.
description_view_3: A narrow end view emphasizing the linear arrangement and tilt of the panels, with the ribbed texture of the platform prominent.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: roof-mounted or platform-mounted with visible metal (likely aluminum) frame
